message: Jake
Detective Maurice
Excuse me,
Hey,
what's going on today?
I want to meet Meryl. She's here, right?
Sorry, but she said she doesn't want to see anyone,
so she's refusing to meet.
Did someone come to see her?
Yeah, well, a few people. Sorry, I'm busy right now. Well, I'll take my leave.
Excuse me,
Hey,
what's the matter,
? Do you need something else?

This is basically the front of the police station.
There’s a counter set up for uniformed officers to assist citizens.
Among the uniformed officers,
stands.
He’s in his usual worn-out coat and wrinkled shirt. The tie is definitely one he’s been using for years.
Today, his partner, that lively guy, isn’t with him. It’s certain a detective wouldn’t act alone, so he must be in the station.
From his face, which has endured many storms over the years, you can see Maurice's cunning and his skill as a detective.
Every day, every day, new cases just keep coming.
What a nasty world.
It really is. So, what’s the matter this time? You can’t meet Meryl.
The only one who can see Meryl right now is her lawyer. It’s the rule, so don’t take it the wrong way.
How is Meryl doing? Is she in a panic or anything?
Surprisingly, she’s keeping her cool. Most suspects lose their composure and start causing a scene.
But if she’s behaving normally, that’s fine.
Now then, she looks normal on the outside, but...
It’s useless no matter how much you ask, you can’t meet Meryl.
Meryl has confessed. She's being unnaturally honest, it's scary.
Confessed to what, exactly?
To the murder of Guy and Goldstein. She claims she avenged her daughter.
I can understand the feelings of parents who have lost a child, but...
But really, she...
No, never mind.
Are you saying you doubt she did it?
No, not at all.
Not at all.
Well, since Meryl herself acknowledged it, there's nothing we can do, right?
Ah, so you're talking about that room in the decoy house you mentioned. There was nothing in that room.
What!? We didn't move anything and left it as it was.
Indeed, there were significant blood traces found in that room. But the coffin-like thing you mentioned was nowhere to be seen.
No way.
Maybe it was taken away before we arrived. Even if reluctantly, we had plenty of time to do so.
I wonder what you actually saw.
So, that was where the perpetrator of the serial murder case neatly stored the dismembered body parts.
But the first victim was killed six months ago. It's strange to think their body could still exist without decaying.
Maybe you were just dreaming?
I looked into the owner of that decoy house room you told me about, but that person has already gone missing.
I have a feeling the owner of that room is the most likely suspect; what do you think?
Do you really think that person did it?
It's often the case that a seemingly incomprehensible crime involves someone you wouldn’t expect.
There are many times where the culprit is such a person.
But...
I understand your feelings, but it's my duty to catch the real culprit.
No matter how minimal the possibility, I cannot ignore it.
A pervert is roaming the streets, indiscriminately mutilating women to satisfy his warped desires.
The citizens' complaints against the police, who can't catch the culprit, must be quite intense. Maurice's face is heavily marked by fatigue.
Even a veteran detective on this road for decades can't capture the perpetrator of a bizarre murder.
His face and the clothes he's wearing look exactly like a detective pulled straight out of a novel. For Maurice, being a detective must be a calling.
If only it had been understood a bit earlier that Guy was not the murderer of Cindy, Meryl could have escaped the murder charge.
Not only did Guy kill, but even Kristy was taken with him. Was the killer that hated Cindy so much?
Meryl probably has no regrets even if she gets caught. She believes she was able to punish the one who killed her daughter with her own hands.
A horrifying creation made of the countless bloodstains and the bodies of the victimized women. That has to be the murder scene.
If the Decoy House is the murder scene,
the likelihood that the owner of that room is the culprit is very strong. I don’t want to believe it, but.
That bloodstained room, like in a coffin,
created by a mentally disturbed creator,
I can't forget that artistic work.
Did the culprit kill innocent women to make that thing found in the coffin of the Decoy House?
The police must think the owner of the room with bloodstains is the culprit. That means she is.
Could the tenant of the Decoy House be the bizarre murder perpetrator?
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
